strengths: Strong distributed-systems and high-throughput backend pedigree: low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second maps directly to the real-time inference and event-streaming demands of the role.
Hands-on exposure to agentic AI tooling and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, demonstrating early but genuine engagement with the technical domain the role requires.
Consistent software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) and community knowledge-sharing indicate the engineering-culture-building instincts needed at the leadership level.
concerns: No demonstrated people-management or organizational leadership: the entire career record is individual-contributor and staff-engineer roles; there is no evidence of managing engineering teams, let alone scaling one from 10 to 30+ with a senior leadership layer.

Agentic AI experience is shallow in scope and duration—six months of tooling contributions at re:cinq does not constitute the production, multi-agent, mission-critical AI systems operation the role treats as a hard requirement.

Fintech infrastructure depth is superficial: RIDE Capital and Optilyz are tangential (investment-admin tooling and direct-mail pricing) rather than core credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk, or working-capital management.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically skilled and versatile individual contributor with a credible distributed-systems background and nascent exposure to agentic tooling, but he does not meet the executive profile this role demands: there is no track record of engineering-org leadership, headcount scaling, board-level communication, or operating AI systems in regulated financial environments with real commercial consequences, and his AI experience—while directionally relevant—is too recent and too narrow to satisfy the role's hard requirement for demonstrated production multi-agent systems at scale.
